quick_skim.title: Efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation via Token Co-occurrence Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) mitigates hallucinations in Large Language Models (LLMs) by grounding the generation process on external knowledge. However, standard RAG approaches struggle with multi-hop reasoning. While recent graph-based RAG methods improve the retrieval of interconnected chunks, they often rely on computationally expensive and error-prone LLM-based extraction pipelines. To address these issues, we propose TIGRAG (Token-Induced GraphRAG), an efficient graph-augmented RAG framework based on a token co-occurrence Knowledge Graph. TIGRAG directly models topological relationships between tokens using sliding-window co-occurrence statistics, thus enabling scalable graph construction. During inference, it combines graph-based semantic expansion and neural reranking to retrieve interconnected evidence for multi-hop reasoning. Specifically, it introduces an iterative entity-driven retrieval strategy that progressively expands the query using bridging entities extracted from previously retrieved contexts. We evaluated TIGRAG on three widely adopted multi-hop Question Answering (QA) benchmarks. Experimental results demonstrated that our framework consistently outperforms dense retrieval and graph-based RAG methods in both retrieval and downstream QA tasks, while substantially reducing indexing time, inference latency, and prompt footprint.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Graph-based RAG method using token co-occurrence KG, applicable to DDI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于令牌共现知识图谱的图检索增强生成方法，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于令牌共现知识图谱的图检索增强生成方法，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于令牌共现知识图谱的图检索增强生成方法，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TIGRAG proposes an efficient graph-augmented RAG framework using token co-occurrence KG, enabling scalable multi-hop reasoning that could be adapted for drug interaction prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 标准RAG在多跳推理中存在局限，而基于图的RAG方法常依赖昂贵的LLM提取管道。本文提出TIGRAG，通过滑动窗口共现统计直接建模令牌间拓扑关系，构建令牌共现知识图谱，在推理时结合图语义检索。该方法高效且可扩展，可迁移至药物相互作用预测等需要多跳推理的场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 标准RAG在多跳推理中存在局限，而基于图的RAG方法常依赖昂贵的LLM提取管道。本文提出TIGRAG，通过滑动窗口共现统计直接建模令牌间拓扑关系，构建令牌共现知识图谱，在推理时结合图语义检索。该方法高效且可扩展，可迁移至药物相互作用预测等需要多跳推理的场景。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找ColdDDI：利用大型语言模型和生物医学知识图谱进行冷启动药物相互作用预测该方向有关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: Large Language Model + Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction; cold-start drug-drug interaction prediction; large language model for drug interaction; drug interaction prediction with knowledge graph; knowledge graph completion for drug discovery; cold start prediction in drug interaction; Cold start drug drug interaction prediction using large language models and biomedical knowledge graphs; Leveraging knowledge graphs for cold start drug interaction with large language models; Biomedical knowledge graph enhanced drug interaction prediction for new drugs.

quick_skim.title: Query-Aware Spreading Activation for Multi-Hop Retrieval over Knowledge Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation built on knowledge graphs (Graph RAG) outperforms flat passage retrieval on multi-hop question answering by leveraging graph structure. In most existing systems, however, the question only sets the seed nodes; the subsequent traversal becomes "query-blind", depending solely on the graph structure. The exception is QAFD-RAG, which implements query-aware traversal via a flow-diffusion solver with combined edge re-weighting. This architecture requires loading the full graph into Python memory and an iterative solver with a variable number of iterations complicating integration with the graph database. We propose a spreading-activation method that achieves the same query-aware traversal with a single per-step semantic gate: the step weight is the cosine similarity between the candidate entity's description and the question, and the number of iterations is fixed. The whole retrieval procedure - seed mapping, propagation, top-K selection and context assembly - is expressed as a single Cypher query executed in one round-trip to Neo4j; the graph never leaves the database. On MuSiQue our method matches QAFD-RAG by exact match (32.80 vs 33.50) and outperforms the strongest purely-structural baseline in our comparison, HippoRAG, by 5.3 EM and 3.4 F1; on 2WikiMultiHopQA HippoRAG and QAFD-RAG retain an advantage due to their phrase-node architectures. An ablation with the gate disabled confirms that the gate is the source of a simultaneous F1 gain of 3.6 to 7.4 points and a retrieval-latency reduction by a factor of 1.5 to 4.9.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Query-aware KG traversal method for multi-hop retrieval, applicable to DDI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 查询感知的知识图谱多跳检索方法，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 查询感知的知识图谱多跳检索方法，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 查询感知的知识图谱多跳检索方法，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces a spreading-activation method for query-aware traversal over KGs, enabling efficient multi-hop retrieval that could enhance drug interaction knowledge extraction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有图RAG系统在种子节点后遍历过程是查询盲的，缺乏语义引导。本文提出一种查询感知的传播激活方法，每步进行单次语义检查，实现与流扩散求解器相当的效果但更易集成。该方法可直接用于从知识图谱中检索药物相互作用相关的多跳路径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有图RAG系统在种子节点后遍历过程是查询盲的，缺乏语义引导。本文提出一种查询感知的传播激活方法，每步进行单次语义检查，实现与流扩散求解器相当的效果但更易集成。该方法可直接用于从知识图谱中检索药物相互作用相关的多跳路径。
